title: Physical ID Assignment Config Schema
description: Defines the allowed structure and required fields for the physical identity assignment configuration.

repository_root.description: Absolute path to the repository root where IDs should be assigned.

excluded_directories.description: List of directory names or relative paths to exclude from physical ID assignment.

excluded_files.description: List of file name patterns to exclude from physical ID assignment (e.g., file extensions or glob-like patterns).

assign_root_directory_id.description: If true the repository root will receive its own directory_id; if false the root is omitted from the ID namespace.

directory_identity_mechanism.description: Specification of how directories are identified.

mechanism.description: Mechanism used to persist directory identity. Only 'sidecar_anchor_file' is supported.

anchor_filename.description: Name of the sidecar file used as the directory anchor (e.g., '.dir_id').

anchor_required.description: Whether every non-excluded directory must contain an anchor file.

file_identity_mechanism.description: Specification of how files are assigned unique identifiers.

mechanism.description: Method used to assign file identities. 'registry_assigned' means IDs are assigned by the registry and not embedded in filenames; 'filename_prefix' means IDs are embedded at the start of filenames.

rename_required.description: If true the file names will be mutated to embed the file_id; if false IDs are stored only in the registry.

physical_id_patterns.description: Defines prefix and length patterns for physical identifiers.

id_length.description: Total number of digits in physical identifiers.

file_id_pattern.description: Regular expression pattern that all file_id values must match.

directory_id_pattern.description: Regular expression pattern that all directory_id values must match.
